blocks.title: What is Alloy? When does it make sense to use it?

blocks.content: Grafana Alloy combines the strengths of leading telemetry collectors into one place. Whether you observe applications, infrastructure, or both, Alloy can collect, process, and export telemetry signals so your observability approach can scale.
The following diagram shows Alloy as a single point for collecting and forwarding telemetry data.

blocks.alt: Diagram showing how Alloy collects telemetry signals and forwards them to databases and other systems

blocks.content:
Alloy is often a strong fit when you need to:

- Collect metrics, logs, traces, and profiles with one collector instead of several agents
- Use both Prometheus and OpenTelemetry ecosystems without choosing only one
- Keep vendor flexibility by sending data to Grafana Cloud, a self-managed Grafana stack, or other compatible backends
- Start simple and grow into clustering, remote configuration, and shared pipeline modules as your needs expand
In the next milestone, you learn how Alloy fits in an observability architecture and how its engines relate.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your path, you can explore the following topics:

- [Introduction to Grafana Alloy](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/introduction/)
- [Why Grafana Alloy](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/introduction/why-alloy/)
- [Grafana Alloy for Beginners series](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLDGkOdUX1UjoUmd6Z-lKgGaGzmZvYxRWs)
